finding: Mean age: 41 years; extremely rare in infants + children

differentials.name: Leiomyosarcoma (1-9%): predilection for left atrium; tends to invade pulmonary veins + mitral valve
differentials.raw: Leiomyosarcoma (1-9%): predilection for left atrium; tends to invade pulmonary veins + mitral valve

differentials.name: dyspnea, pericardial tamponade, arrhythmia, syncope, peripheral edema, sudden death
differentials.raw: dyspnea, pericardial tamponade, arrhythmia, syncope, peripheral edema, sudden death

differentials.name: embolic phenomena, chest pain, pneumonia, fever
differentials.raw: embolic phenomena, chest pain, pneumonia, fever

differentials.name: chest pain, right heart failure, supraventricular arrhythmia
differentials.raw: chest pain, right heart failure, supraventricular arrhythmia

differentials.name: fever, weight loss; pericardial tamponade = bloody fluid on pericardiocentesis
differentials.raw: fever, weight loss; pericardial tamponade = bloody fluid on pericardiocentesis (rarely with malignant cells)

context: (a)  right-sided heart inflow obstruction (tumor in right atrium) (b)  mitral valve obstruction (tumor in left atrium) Age:   5-10 years earlier than other sarcomas DDx:   myxoma (at fossa ovalis) √  contrast enhancement, areas of hemorrhage, central necrosis CXR: √  cardiomegaly, RA enlargement √  CHF √  pleural effusion, pericardial effusion √  focal cardiac mass √  pulmonary consolidation PET: √  difficult identification of sarcoma if myocardial FDG activity is high Metastatic to:   lung, lymph nodes, bone, liver, brain, bowel, spleen, adrenal gland, pleura, diaphragm, kidney, thyroid, skin
